action_choice_hints: Action cell is in the same row or column as a special target token.
Action cell is the nearest unblocked cell to a target token along a straight line.
There exists a contiguous segment of empty cells between the action cell and a boundary or obstacle.
Action cell is adjacent to a moving token or agent.
Action cell is within K steps of a target token, where K is small.
Action cell is the first empty cell encountered in a given direction from a moving token.
Action cell is not blocked by obstacles when moving from a moving token toward a target.
Action cell is part of a contiguous run of empty cells extending toward a target token.
Action cell is in a region (e.g., top/bottom band) containing a target token.
Action cell is the only available cell in a direction from a moving token before hitting an obstacle.
Action cell is aligned with the direction indicated by a directional token.
Action cell is the next cell along the path from a moving token to a target token.
Action cell is not adjacent to any obstacle.
Action cell is the first cell after a drawn trace in a given direction.
Action cell is in a column or row with no obstacles between it and a target token.
Action cell is the only cell in a contiguous empty segment between two obstacles.
transition_hints: Selecting an action cell causes a moving token or agent to move to that cell.
A drawn trace is created along the path from the previous position to the action cell.
Obstacles remain static and do not change between transitions.
Empty cells become part of a drawn trace when traversed by the moving token.
Target tokens may disappear or change state when reached by the moving token.
Directional tokens may influence the direction of movement for the agent.
The agent's previous position becomes part of the drawn trace after moving.
Boundaries are impassable and do not change state.
Selecting a cell blocked by an obstacle does not result in movement.
Drawn traces persist and accumulate over time.
Only one moving token or agent is active at a time.
The environment updates deterministically based on the selected action cell.

diversity_requirements: Include templates that capture both local (adjacent cell) and global (row/column/region) relationships.
Ensure templates cover both alignment (row/column) and proximity (within K steps) patterns.
placeholder_usage_limits: Limit the number of templates with more than two placeholders to avoid combinatorial explosion.
Restrict templates that require enumeration over all possible directions to a maximum of four directions.
avoidances: Avoid templates that only check for the presence of a single token type without relational context.
Avoid templates that rely solely on absolute coordinates or specific grid positions.
